evidence: File begins with '# GRC Researcher Agent' on line 1 — no YAML frontmatter block (---) exists anywhere in the file; both name and description fields are absent
description: Agent has no YAML frontmatter block; name and description are both absent, making the agent unregisterable and undiscoverable
suggested_fix: Add frontmatter block with name: grc-researcher and a description field before the # heading

evidence: No --- delimited YAML block present in file; description field is absent
description: Agent has no description in frontmatter; cannot be indexed or surfaced in plugin discovery
suggested_fix: Add description: 'Read-only research agent for deep GRC reference lookups across frameworks, mappings, and audit procedures' to frontmatter

evidence: plugin.json contains only name, description, version, author, repository, license, keywords — no commands, agents, or skills arrays
description: Plugin manifest declares identity metadata but no artifact registrations; commands, agents, and skills may not be loadable by Claude Code if directory-based auto-discovery is not in effect
suggested_fix: Add commands, agents, and skills arrays listing the relative paths of all 24 command files, the grc-researcher agent, and the grc-knowledge skill

evidence: YAML frontmatter contains description but no name field; only --- description: ... --- is present
description: Command frontmatter is missing the required name field; all 24 commands share this same gap
suggested_fix: Add name: review-poam to the YAML frontmatter block

evidence: No allowed-tools: field in frontmatter; command instructs agent to Read multiple reference files but declares no tool contract
description: Command missing allowed-tools declaration; all 24 commands share this gap
suggested_fix: Add allowed-tools: [Read, Glob] to frontmatter for all review commands; add WebFetch only if external docs are explicitly fetched

evidence: No model: field present anywhere in the file; no frontmatter block exists
description: Agent declares no model tier; executor cannot select appropriate model for research tasks
suggested_fix: Add model: claude-sonnet-4-6 (or equivalent sonnet-tier) to agent frontmatter

evidence: File has ## Example Research Tasks (6 input queries) but no ## Output Format section defining response structure
description: Agent defines behavior and examples but no output format; responses are structurally undefined
suggested_fix: Add ## Output Format section describing how research responses should be structured (citations, confidence flags, section headings)

evidence: Output Format section reads: 'Varies by action. Always include: Step-by-step instructions, Required fields/evidence, Framework-specific requirements, Examples where helpful' — no concrete template for any of the 6 actions
description: Output format is described only as a list of categories with no template; no action-specific format is defined
suggested_fix: Add a concrete markdown template for each action (create, update, close, template, escalate, metrics) showing the expected section structure

evidence: Line 40: 'Read the appropriate reference files' — 'appropriate' is an unanchored vague quantifier
description: 'appropriate' used without qualification; the referenced files are deterministic from the framework argument and should be listed explicitly
suggested_fix: Replace with explicit conditional: 'For fedramp: read audits/document-section-requirements.md and conmon/poam-management.md'

evidence: Line 39: 'Read the appropriate reference files'; line 45: 'A realistic scenario appropriate to the type and system context' — two vague uses
description: 'appropriate' appears twice; both usages can be made specific
suggested_fix: Line 39: list the two specific reference files; line 45: replace 'appropriate' with 'matched to' or specify concrete scenario attributes per type

evidence: Reference Navigation section: 'read the appropriate reference file' without further qualification
description: 'appropriate' used in the navigation guidance; reference selection criteria are defined elsewhere in the file but the vague phrasing weakens the instruction
suggested_fix: Replace with 'read the reference file listed below for this topic' pointing to the table already present

evidence: readFileSync(skillPath, 'utf-8') reads SKILL.md verbatim; content is concatenated into bootstrap string and injected via experimental.chat.system.transform on every conversation
description: SKILL.md content is read and injected into the AI system prompt without sanitization or size cap; a tampered SKILL.md could inject adversarial instructions into all GRC sessions
suggested_fix: Strip any <IMPORTANT> or </IMPORTANT> tags from skillContent; add a max-length guard (e.g., 20000 chars) before concatenation; document that SKILL.md is a trusted-input boundary

evidence: return { 'experimental.chat.system.transform': async (system) => { ... } } — key is prefixed 'experimental'
description: Plugin registers on an experimental API key with no fallback; if the key is renamed or removed in a future OpenCode release, the plugin silently provides no GRC context with no error
suggested_fix: Wrap registration in a capability check or add a console.warn if the returned object's key is not consumed; document the OpenCode version dependency

evidence: plugin.json has no commands, agents, or skills arrays; 24 command files, 1 agent file, and 1 skill file exist on disk but are not declared in the manifest
description: All 26 NL artifact files on disk are orphaned from the plugin manifest; installation may not expose any slash commands to Claude Code users depending on discovery mechanism
suggested_fix: Add commands array listing grc/commands/*.md paths, agents array listing grc/agents/grc-researcher.md, and skills array listing grc/skills/grc-knowledge/SKILL.md
